title: Lesson 0: Setting Up Scratch Accounts
description: Students explore the Scratch environment and learn how to log into their Scratch accounts.
objective_bullets: Introduce the Scratch environment and learn how to log into their Scratch accounts

title: Lesson 1.1: Computer Science & Scratch Basics
description: Students discuss the elements of a computer and learn about the field of computer science. Students learn the components of the Scratch programming interface, which include coding blocks, scripts, and stage.
objective_bullets: Discuss the elements of a computer and learn about the field of computer science|Learn the components of the Scratch programming interface, which include coding blocks, scripts, and stage

title: Lesson 1.2: Scratch Charades
description: Students play a Scratch version of charades and determine the script that is performed by the actor. They learn how block-based programming works without the complexity of the Scratch platform.
objective_bullets: Play a Scratch version of charades and determine the script that is performed by the actor|Learn how block-based programming works without the complexity of the Scratch platform

title: Lesson 1.3: TIPP & SEE: Scavenger Hunt
description: Students learn the TIPP&SEE framework as a guide to observe, learn and explore.
objective_bullets: Learn the TIPP&SEE framework as a guide to observe, learn and explore

title: Lesson 1.4: Quest Under the Sea
description: Students learn to remix, share, add a project to a class studio. They also modify a project based on requirements from the Quest.
objective_bullets: Learn to remix, share, add a project to a class studio|Also modify a project based on requirements from the Quest

title: Lesson 2.1: Intro to Sequence
description: Students explore the computer science concept of sequence by participation in an unplugged hands-on activity.
objective_bullets: Introduce the computer science concept of sequence by participation in an unplugged hands-on activity

title: Lesson 2.2: Exploring Sequence with TIPP&SEE
description: Students use the TIPP&SEE strategy to play, predict and explore sequencing within an existing project. They observe the actions of sprites (move, play sound, say or think something) that run in the proper order.
objective_bullets: Use the TIPP&SEE strategy to play, predict and explore sequencing within an existing project|Observe the actions of sprites (move, play sound, say or think something) that run in the proper order

title: Lesson 2.3: Quest & Name Poem: Plan
description: Students modify an existing project by adding a sequence of actions to sprites to complete their Quest. They also explore through a Name Poem project. They plan what they want their sprites to do before building their project.
objective_bullets: Modify an existing project by adding a sequence of actions to sprites to complete their Quest|Explore through a Name Poem project|Plan what they want their sprites to do before building their project

title: Lesson 2.4 Name Poem: Build
description: Students build their Name Poem project. They create sequences of blocks that contain one event block and at least one action block.
objective_bullets: Build their Name Poem project|Create sequences of blocks that contain one event block and at least one action block

title: Lesson 2.5: Name Poem - Reflect and Share
description: Students reflect on their own Name Poem projects, then share their projects with a partner.
objective_bullets: On their own Name Poem projects, then share their projects with a partner

title: Lesson 3.1: Intro to Events
description: Students learn how Events act as commands that can trigger one or multiple actions to occur at the same time.
objective_bullets: Learn how Events act as commands that can trigger one or multiple actions to occur at the same time

title: Lesson 3.2: Exploring Events with TIPP&SEE
description: The concept of Events in programming is introduced. Students use the TIPP&SEE strategy to play, predict and explore an existing Scratch project that contains basic events.
objective_bullets: Explore Events in programming is introduced|The TIPP&SEE strategy to play, predict and explore an existing Scratch project that contains basic events

title: Lesson 3.3: Quest & About Me - Plan
description: Students modify an existing project by adding event scripts to complete their Quest. Students also think through how Events work as they plan their About Me project. Students plan what they want their sprites in their program to do.
objective_bullets: Modify an existing project by adding event scripts to complete their Quest|Also think through how Events work as they plan their About Me project|Plan what they want their sprites in their program to do

title: Lesson 3.4: About Me - Build
description: Students build their About Me project from nine Scratch blocks that students have been introduced to.
objective_bullets: Build their About Me project from nine Scratch blocks that learners have been introduced to

title: Lesson 3.5: About Me - Reflect and Share
description: Students reflect on their own About Me projects, then share their projects with two partners.
objective_bullets: On their own About Me projects, then share their projects with two partners

title: Lesson 4.1: Intro to Loops
description: Students play an unplugged game, Scratch Charades, to introduce themselves to using Scratch loop blocks in scripts.
objective_bullets: Play an unplugged game, Scratch Charades, to introduce themselves to using Scratch loop blocks in scripts

title: Lesson 4.2: Exploring Loops with TIPP&SEE
description: Students modify a Scratch project to animate animals in a parade using loops.
objective_bullets: Explore a Scratch project to animate animals in a parade using loops

title: Lesson 4.3: Quest & Dance Party Plan
description: Students explore through a Dance Party project using loops. Students plan what they want their sprites to do before building their project.
objective_bullets: Explore through a Dance Party project using loops|Plan what they want their sprites to do before building their project

title: Lesson 4.4: Dance Party Build
description: Students build their own Dance Party project.
objective_bullets: Build their own Dance Party project

title: Lesson 4.5: Project Share Out & Reflection
description: Students reflect on their own Dance Party projects, then share their projects with a partner. objective_bullets: On their own Dance Party projects, then share their projects with a partner

title: Lesson 1.1: Scratch Basics, Sequence and Events Review
description: Students review Scratch Basics skills and the CS Concepts of Sequence & Event.
objective_bullets: Review Scratch Basics skills and the CS Concepts of Sequence & Event

title: Lesson 1.2: Animation: Unplugged & TIPP&SEE
description: The concepts of Loop & Animation are introduced through an unplugged “Flipbook” activity and by completing a TIPP&SEE for an existing animation project.
objective_bullets: Explore Loop & Animation through an unplugged “Flipbook” activity and by completing a TIPP&SEE for an existing animation project

title: Lesson 1.3: Quest & Animated Story: Plan
description: Students modify an existing project by adding and changing repeat loops to complete a “Quest.” Students also think through how Animation works as they plan their Animation Story Project.
objective_bullets: Modify an existing project by adding and changing repeat loops to complete a “Quest.” Also think through how Animation works as they plan their Animation Story Project

title: Lesson 1.4: Animated Story - Build
description: Students build their Animation Story Project.
objective_bullets: Build their Animation Story Project

title: Lesson 1.5: Animated Story - Reflect & Share
description: Students reflect on their own Animation Story Project, then share their project with partners.
objective_bullets: On their own Animation Story Project, then share their project with partners

title: Lesson 2.1: Intro to Loops with Conditions
description: Students explore the computer science concept of Loops with Conditions and will play an unplugged, hands-on activity.
objective_bullets: Introduce the computer science concept of Loops with Conditions and play an unplugged, hands-on activity

title: Lesson 2.2: Exploring Loops with Conditions
description: Students explore a Scratch Project to identify how Loops with Conditions work and the types of sensing conditions that are used in loops.
objective_bullets: Explore a Scratch project to identify how Loops with Conditions work and the types of sensing conditions that are used in loops

title: Lesson 2.3: Loops with Conditions: Quest & Plan
description: Students modify an existing project and plan a new project using loops with conditions.
objective_bullets: An existing project and plan a new project using loops with conditions

title: Lesson 2.4: Loops with Conditions: Build Project
description: Students complete a Debugging Challenge and build their new loops with conditions project.
objective_bullets: A Debugging Challenge and build their new loops with conditions project

title: Lesson 2.5: Loops with Conditions: Reflect and Share
description: Students reflect on their own projects, then share their projects with a partner/class. Students learn about a Memorable Computer Scientist.
objective_bullets: On their own projects, then share their projects with a partner/class|About a Memorable Computer Scientist

title: Lesson 3.1: Exploring Parallelism with TIPP&SEE
description: Students explore the concept of parallelism by participating in an unplugged class activity and by completing a TIPP&SEE for an existing project.
objective_bullets: Introduce the concept of parallelism by participating in an unplugged class activity and by completing a TIPP&SEE for an existing project

title: Lesson 3.2: Exploring Synchronization with TIPP&SEE
description: Students explore the concept of synchronization by participating in an unplugged class activity and by completing a TIPP&SEE for an existing project.
objective_bullets: Introduce the concept of synchronization by participating in an unplugged class activity and by completing a TIPP&SEE for an existing project

title: Lesson 3.3: Parallelism & Synchronization: Quest & Plan
description: First, students modify the parallel path/synchronization existing project. Then, students plan for a new open-ended project that uses parallelism and synchronization.
objective_bullets: First, modify the parallel path/synchronization existing project|Then, plan for a new open-ended project that uses parallelism and synchronization

title: Lesson 3.4: Parallelism & Synchronization: Build
description: Students use their planning guides to build their project in Scratch. The project focuses on using parallelism & synchronization.
objective_bullets: Use their planning guides to build their project in Scratch|The project focuses on using parallelism & synchronization

title: Lesson 3.5: Parallelism & Synchronization: Share & Reflect
description: Students reflect on their own projects, then share their projects with a partner.
objective_bullets: On their own projects, then share their projects with a partner

title: Lesson 4.1: Intro to Variables
description: Students explore the concept of variables through the VARIO unplugged card game.
objective_bullets: Explore variables through the VARIO unplugged card game

title: Lesson 4.2: Exploring Variables with TIPP&SEE
description: Students explore a Scratch project to understand the variables concept in context using a TIPP&SEE activity.
objective_bullets: Explore a Scratch project to understand the variables concept in context using a TIPP&SEE activity

title: Lesson 4.3: Variables: Quest & Plan
description: Students modify an existing project and then work in pairs to plan a new project using variables.
objective_bullets: An existing project and then work in pairs to plan a new project using variables

title: Lesson 4.4: Variables: Build
description: Students complete a Debugging Challenge and build their new game with variables project.
objective_bullets: A Debugging Challenge and build their new game with variables project

title: Lesson 4.5: Variables: Share & Reflect
description: Students reflect on their own projects, then share their projects with a partner. Students learn about a Memorable Computer Scientist. Contact Us at ECforALL@uci.edu Back to Top This curriculum is supported in part by the National Science Foundation through Grants #2317832 and #1923136 and by the United States Department of Education through Grant #U411C190092. ©2022 by ECforALL.
objective_bullets: On their own projects, then share their projects with a partner|About a Memorable Computer Scientist

title: Lesson 1.1 Setting Up Scratch Accounts
description: Students create and log into a new Scratch account. Students explore the Scratch environment. Students learn about role model Ada Lovelace.
objective_bullets: Create and log into a new Scratch account|Introduce the Scratch environment|Learn about role model Ada Lovelace

title: Lesson 1.2: Introduction to Scratch
description: Students explore parts of the Scratch environment. They learn how to add their projects to a Scratch students and how to edit edit and import an image in Scratch.
objective_bullets: Explore parts of the Scratch environment|Learn how to add their projects to a Scratch learners and how to edit edit and import an image in Scratch

title: Lesson 1.3: Scratch Charades
description: Students familiarize themselves with coding commands, specialfically in the Scratch language, in an unplugged activity acting the scripts out. Students enjoy playing this version of the familiar charades game.
objective_bullets: Familiarize themselves with coding commands, specialfically in the Scratch language, in an unplugged activity acting the scripts out|Enjoy playing this version of the familiar charades game

title: Lesson 1.4: Coding Concepts 1 - Sequence, Algorithm, Program
description: Students explore the concepts of sequence, algorithm, and program. They learn the difference between the “glide” and “go to” Scratch block. They debug an existing project. Finaly, they tinker in Scratch.
objective_bullets: Introduce the concepts of sequence, algorithm, and program|Learn the difference between the “glide” and “go to” Scratch block|Debug an existing project|Finaly, tinker in Scratch

title: Lesson 1.5: Coding Concepts 2 - Events, Loops, Animation
description: Students learn about events, loops, and animation. They learn that animation is a series of images that are in a loop. They gain an understanding of the concept from a lecture, from videos, from debugging and from making their own flipbook.
objective_bullets: Learn about events, loops, and animation|Learn that animation is a series of images that are in a loop|Gain an understanding of the concept from a lecture, from videos, from debugging and from making their own flipbook

title: Lesson 1.6: Broadcasting and Receiving
description: Students learn how to time interactions in Scratch by using the “broadcast” and “receiving” block. This skill enables students to time interactions between characters when creating a digital story or when including narration or music in a project.
objective_bullets: Learn how to time interactions in Scratch by using the “broadcast” and “receiving” block|This skill enables time interactions between characters when creating a digital story or when including narration or music in a project

title: Lesson 2.1: What is a system?
description: Students explore the concepts of natural and human systems. They play a card game with their classmates identifying how parts of the chaparral ecosystem are related.
objective_bullets: Explore natural and human systems|They play a card game with their classmates identifying how parts of the chaparral ecosystem are related

title: Lesson 2.2 Disruptions in an Ecosystem
description: Students learn about invasive species through the context of the chaparral ecosystem. They create a public service in Scratch to teach others about what they learn.
objective_bullets: About invasive species through the context of the chaparral ecosystem|Create a public service in Scratch to teach others about what they learn

title: Lesson 2.3: Demonstrating Interactions in Scratch
description: Students understand the computing concept of parallelism, initialization, and loops that they need to understand to complete the connection circle project in the next lesson.
objective_bullets: Understand the computing concept of parallelism, initialization, and loops that needed to complete the connection circle project in the next lesson

title: Lesson 2.4: Modeling an Ecosystem
description: Students code a connection circle in Scratch to model an ecosystem of their choice.
objective_bullets: Code a connection circle in Scratch to model an ecosystem of their choice

title: Lesson 2.5: Reflect and Share
description: Students reflect on their Connection Circle projects, then share their projects with a partner.
objective_bullets: On their Connection Circle projects, then share their projects with a partner

title: Lesson 3.2: Use, Create, and Set Variables with TIPP&SEE
description: Students do a TIPP&SEE activity to reinforce the concepts from lesson 3.1. They are guided as they modify an existing project.
objective_bullets: Do a TIPP&SEE activity to reinforce the concepts from lesson 3.1|They are guided as they modify an existing project

title: Lesson 3.3: Plan, Debug, Build and Share
description: Students plan a Madlibs project, do a debugging activity as a class, then build their Madlibs projects. When finished, students test each other’s projects.
objective_bullets: Plan a Madlibs project, do a debugging activity as a class, then build their Madlibs projects|When finished, learners test each other’s projects

title: Lesson 3.4: Intro to Loops with if, then, else Conditionals
description: Students explore the computer science concept of if, then, else conditions and will play an unplugged, hands-on activity.
objective_bullets: Introduce the computer science concept of if, then, else conditions and play an unplugged, hands-on activity

title: Lesson 3.5: Exploring Loops with if, then else Conditionals
description: Students explore a Scratch Project to identify how if, then else conditions work and the types of operators that are used in conditions. Students modify an existing project.
objective_bullets: Explore a Scratch project to identify how if, then else conditions work and the types of operators that are used in conditions|An existing project

title: Lesson 3.6: Introduction to Functions
description: Students explore the computer science concept of functions, play an unplugged activity, and explore a Scratch project with functions.
objective_bullets: Introduce the computer science concept of functions, play an unplugged activity, and explore a Scratch project with functions

title: Lesson 3.7: Exploring Functions: Quest & Plan
description: Students explore a Scratch Project to identify how functions work and how to identify code that can be converted into functions. Students modify an existing project and plan for their Create project.
objective_bullets: Explore a Scratch project to identify how functions work and how to identify code that can be converted into functions|An existing project and plan for their Create project

title: Lesson 3.8: Functions and if, then, else Conditional Loops: Plan & Build
description: Students complete a project using functions and if, then else conditions. Students complete a Debugging Challenge then complete the scripts and create functions for a graphing project.
objective_bullets: A project using functions and if, then else conditions|A Debugging Challenge then complete the scripts and create functions for a graphing project

title: Lesson 4.1: We Need Trees
description: Students explain how trees benefit a community and identify reasons the number of trees is decreasing each year.
objective_bullets: Be explain how trees benefit a community and identify reasons the number of trees is decreasing each year

title: Lesson 4.2: Tree Equity Score
description: Students explore the treeequityscore website. They compare the tree equity score and heat disparity scores in different zip codes. They discuss what data is and how it can be visualized.
objective_bullets: Introduce the treeequityscore website|Compare the tree equity score and heat disparity scores in different zip codes|Discuss what data is and how it can be visualized

title: Lesson 4.3: Quantitative and Qualitative Data
description: Students explore what data is. They compare qualitative data versus quantitative data. They learn the appropriate data visualization to represent data.
objective_bullets: Introduce what data is|Compare qualitative data versus quantitative data|Learn the appropriate data visualization to represent data

title: Lesson 4.4: Creating a Survey on Trees
description: Students develop a survey on the topic of trees to learn the perspectives on a particular part of their community. They identify people who would be interested in the finding of this proposed research.
objective_bullets: Develop a survey on the topic of trees to learn the perspectives on a particular part of their community|Identify people who would be interested in the finding of this proposed research

title: Lesson 4.5: Collecting and Cleaning Data
description: Students conduct interviews to gather their data.
objective_bullets: Conduct interviews to gather their data

title: Lesson 4.6: Visualizing and Analyzing Data
description: Students create a visualization with their data and discuss what it means. They discuss pros and cons of different ways that data can be represented
objective_bullets: Create a visualization with their data and discuss what it means|Discuss pros and cons of different ways that data can be represented

title: Lesson 5.1: What is a Public Service Announcement (PSA)?
description: Students view public service announcements and advertisements as they identify the problems being addressed, the supporting facts, the call to do something, and the kind of persuasion they represent. (50 min)
objective_bullets: View public service announcements and advertisements as they identify the problems being addressed, the supporting facts, the call to do something, and the kind of persuasion they represent|(50 min)

title: Lesson 5.2: Crafting a Message
description: Students create a relevant message for the community supported with the findings of their survey. The message will include a behavioral request from the audience. (90 min)
objective_bullets: A relevant message for the community supported with the findings of their survey|The message will include a behavioral request from the audience|(90 min)

title: Lesson 5.3: Build Your Public Service Announcement (PSA)
description: Students create a PSA in the Scratch environment in small groups that includes a data visualization that was created in Unit 4. (50 min)
objective_bullets: A PSA in the Scratch environment in small groups that includes a data visualization that was created in Unit 4|(50 min)

title: Lesson 5.4: Sharing Your Public Service Announcement (PSA)
description: Students self-reflect on their projects. They give helpful feedback on classmate’s projects and accept feedback from others. Desired changes are made. Projects are shared with members of the community. (30 min) Contact Us at ECforALL@uci.... ©2022 by ECforALL.
objective_bullets: Self-reflect on their projects|They give helpful feedback on classmate’s projects and accept feedback from others|Desired changes are made|Projects are shared with members of the community

title: Lesson 0.1 - Getting Started with CreatiCode
description: Create and sign in to a CreatiCode account using email/password or Google. Explore community projects and understand project statistics. Navigate "My Stuff" and manage personal projects. Recognize how to remix, share, and publish projects. Effectively use the block palette and project search features.
objective_bullets: Create and sign in to a CreatiCode account using email/password or Google|Explore community projects and understand project statistics|Navigate "My Stuff" and manage personal projects|Recognize how to remix, share, and publish projects

title: Lesson 0.2 - Build Your First Scene
description: Identify and describe key components of the CreatiCode interface including sprite, sprite pane, stage, backdrop, code area, code block, etc. Add and customize sprites. Select and apply different backdrops. Understand the purpose of the green flag and stop sign. Save and share their own projects.
objective_bullets: Identify and describe key components of the CreatiCode interface including sprite, sprite pane, stage, backdrop, code area, code block, etc|Add and customize sprites|Select and apply different backdrops|Understand the purpose of the green flag and stop sign

title: Lesson 0.3 - Build Your First Game
description: Understand the concept of programming as giving instructions to a computer. Identify and use basic motion and control blocks in CreatiCode. Use conditional “if then” and sensing “touching” blocks Use a loop to repeat actions. Add a score variable to track game progress Build an interactive game where a cat moves to collect apples.
objective_bullets: Understand the concept of programming as giving instructions to a computer|Identify and use basic motion and control blocks in CreatiCode|Use conditional “if then” and sensing “touching” blocks Use a loop to repeat actions|Add a score variable to track game progress Build an interactive game where a cat moves to collect apples

title: Lesson 1.1: Intro to AI & CreatiCode
description: Understand the basic types of Artificial Intelligence (Generative and Predictive AI). Recognize real-world applications and the significance of AI in modern society. Interact with AI tools within the CreatiCode platform, including ChatGPT ("Chat with Einstein") and CreatiCode XO. Develop an appreciation for the role of AI in changing the world and its ethical implications.
objective_bullets: Understand the basic types of Artificial Intelligence (Generative and Predictive AI)|Recognize real-world applications and the significance of AI in modern society|Interact with AI tools within the CreatiCode platform, including ChatGPT ("Chat with Einstein") and CreatiCode XO|Develop an appreciation for the role of AI in changing the world and its ethical implications

title: Lesson 1.2: Searching/Generating Backdrops with AI
description: Understand how to search for existing AI-generated backdrops in the CreatiCode library. Learn to create custom backdrops using detailed text prompts. Develop skills in crafting effective prompts for AI image generation. Recognize the importance of refining prompts and iterating to achieve desired results.
objective_bullets: Understand how to search for existing AI-generated backdrops in the CreatiCode library|Learn to create custom backdrops using detailed text prompts|Develop skills in crafting effective prompts for AI image generation|Recognize the importance of refining prompts and iterating to achieve desired results

title: Lesson 1.3: Searching/Generating Sprites with AI
description: Learn how to search for existing AI-generated sprites using appropriate categories. Develop skills in crafting detailed prompts for generating custom sprites. Practice refining queries to achieve desired sprite characteristics.
objective_bullets: Learn how to search for existing AI-generated sprites using appropriate categories|Develop skills in crafting detailed prompts for generating custom sprites|Practice refining queries to achieve desired sprite characteristics

title: Lesson 1.4: Project: "Journey of a Waterdrop"
description: Apply AI Tools: Utilize AI-generated sprites and backdrops to create simple animations; use the image editing tool to generate new variations of images or fix issues. Storytelling: Develop a basic narrative through visual and textual elements. Prompt Engineering: Enhance skills in crafting effective prompts for AI image generation. Basic Coding: Implement fundamental coding blocks to control sprite actions and scene transitions. Creativity: Foster creativity by designing and presenting a simple AI-driven story.
objective_bullets: Apply AI Tools: Utilize AI-generated sprites and backdrops to create simple animations|Use the image editing tool to generate new variations of images or fix issues|Storytelling: Develop a basic narrative through visual and textual elements|Prompt Engineering: Enhance skills in crafting effective prompts for AI image generation

title: Lesson 1.5: Ethics Discussion: AI-generated Images
description: Understand the ethical implications of using AI-generated images. Explore concepts of copyright, licensing, and ownership related to AI art. Recognize the importance of image moderation in K–12 settings. Develop critical thinking skills regarding the responsible use of AI in creative projects.
objective_bullets: Understand the ethical implications of using AI-generated images|Explore concepts of copyright, licensing, and ownership related to AI art|Recognize the importance of image moderation in K–12 settings|Develop critical thinking skills regarding the responsible use of AI in creative projects

title: Lesson 1.6: Learning with CreatiCode XO
description: Understand how to effectively use CreatiCode XO to ask “Explain to me…” and how-to questions about coding blocks or concepts. Develop the ability to ask follow-up questions to maintain context in conversations with XO. Learn to decompose a large challenge into smaller steps Learn to control and customize XO’s response styles through prompt engineering. Learn to handle syntax errors in XO’s response Enhance problem-solving skills by leveraging XO as a coding assistant.
objective_bullets: Understand how to effectively use CreatiCode XO to ask “Explain to me…” and how-to questions about coding blocks or concepts|Develop the ability to ask follow-up questions to maintain context in conversations with XO|Learn to decompose a large challenge into smaller steps Learn to control and customize XO’s response styles through prompt engineering|Learn to handle syntax errors in XO’s response Enhance problem-solving skills by leveraging XO as a coding assistant

title: Lesson 1.7: Debugging with CreatiCode XO
description: Understand debugging as identifying differences between expected and actual outcomes. Master debugging techniques: Logical Deduction, Simplifying Programs, Logging, Step-by-Step Execution, Breakpoints, and Explaining Code to Others. Apply systematic debugging processes to locate and fix bugs independently. Leverage CreatiCode XO effectively to assist the debugging process when stuck. Recognize XO’s limitations and maintain primary responsibility in debugging.
objective_bullets: Understand debugging as identifying differences between expected and actual outcomes|Master debugging techniques: Logical Deduction, Simplifying Programs, Logging, Step-by-Step Execution, Breakpoints, and Explaining Code to Others|Apply systematic debugging processes to locate and fix bugs independently|Leverage CreatiCode XO effectively to assist the debugging process when stuck

title: Lesson 1.8: Using XO to Generate Quizzes
description: Understand how to utilize CreatiCode XO to generate quizzes and provide feedback on our answers. Learn to customize quiz difficulty levels and formats (e.g., multiple-choice, short-answer, coding challenges). Develop skills in crafting effective prompts to obtain meaningful and accurate quizzes. Evaluate the limitations of AI-generated quizzes, particularly in assessing coding challenges. Enhance problem-solving abilities by using XO to check and improve quiz answers.
objective_bullets: Understand how to utilize CreatiCode XO to generate quizzes and provide feedback on our answers|Learn to customize quiz difficulty levels and formats (e.g., multiple-choice, short-answer, coding challenges)|Develop skills in crafting effective prompts to obtain meaningful and accurate quizzes|Evaluate the limitations of AI-generated quizzes, particularly in assessing coding challenges

title: Lesson 1.9: Using XO for Project Design
description: Utilize CreatiCode XO to brainstorm and outline new project ideas. Develop skills in crafting specific and manageable project plans with XO’s assistance. Implement step-by-step project plans by engaging in iterative questioning with XO. Recognize and understand the limitations of XO in project development. Apply best practices in prompt engineering to effectively use XO as a project planning tool.
objective_bullets: Utilize CreatiCode XO to brainstorm and outline new project ideas|Develop skills in crafting specific and manageable project plans with XO’s assistance|Implement step-by-step project plans by engaging in iterative questioning with XO|Recognize and understand the limitations of XO in project development

title: Lesson 1.10: Getting Feedback from XO
description: Understand Importance of Evaluation: Grasp the significance of project evaluation and constructive feedback in the learning process. Utilize Rubrics for Self-Assessment: Learn how to use predefined rubrics to assess their own projects. Interact with CreatiCode XO: Develop skills in using CreatiCode XO to receive automated feedback based on rubrics. Iterative Improvement: Apply feedback from XO to enhance projects through iterative development....
objective_bullets: Understand Importance of Evaluation: Grasp the significance of project evaluation and constructive feedback in the learning process|Utilize Rubrics for Self-Assessment: Learn how to use predefined rubrics to assess their own projects|Interact with CreatiCode XO: Develop skills in using CreatiCode XO to receive automated feedback based on rubrics|Iterative Improvement: Apply feedback from XO to enhance projects through iterative development

title: Lesson 1.11: Generative AI Tools Assessment
description: Recap AI Tools: Reinforce understanding of core AI tools used in the first module. Reflect on Learning: Encourage students to reflect on their experiences and share effective prompts and strategies used with CreatiCode XO. Assess Prompt Engineering Skills: Evaluate students’ ability to craft effective prompts through an assessment.
objective_bullets: Recap AI Tools: Reinforce understanding of core AI tools used in the first module|Reflect on Learning: Encourage reflect on their experiences and share effective prompts and strategies used with CreatiCode XO|Assess Prompt Engineering Skills: Evaluate learners’ ability to craft effective prompts through an assessment

title: Lesson 2.1: Introduction to the ChatGPT Block
description: Identify each parameter of the ChatGPT Request block (prompt, result variable, temperature, length, mode, session). description: This lesson introduces the Scratch environment to students and teaches them to import sprites (characters) and how to edit them.
objective_bullets: Introduce the Scratch environment to learners and teaches them to import sprites (characters) and how to edit them

title: Lesson 3: Scratch Charades
description: Students play a Scratch version of charades and determine the script that is performed by the actor. They learn how block-based programming works without the complexity of the Scratch platform.
objective_bullets: Play a Scratch version of charades and determine the script that is performed by the actor|Learn how block-based programming works without the complexity of the Scratch platform

title: Lesson 4: Coding Concepts 1 - Sequence, Algorithm, Program
description: Students review the concepts of sequence , algorithm, and program to give them language to talk about the process of creating projects. Students debug code to better understand the importance of initialization. They work on a project called "Making Things Move and Talk".
objective_bullets: Review the concepts of sequence , algorithm, and program to give them language to talk about the process of creating projects|Debug code to better understand the importance of initialization|Work on a project called "Making Things Move and Talk"

title: Lesson 5: Coding Concepts 2 - Events, Loops, Animation
description: This lesson introduces the concepts of events, loops, and animation. They debug code to discover the importance of "wait" blocks in animation. Students make a flip book to help students development their conception of animation.
objective_bullets: Introduce the concepts of events, loops, and animation|Debug code to discover the importance of "wait" blocks in animation|Make a flip book to help learners development their conception of animation

title: Lesson 6: Broadcasting and Receiving
description: This lesson introduces students to the concept of broadcasting and receiving. This is an important concept that enables sprites (characters) to interact.
objective_bullets: Introduce the concept of broadcasting and receiving|This is an important concept that enables sprites (characters) to interact

title: Name Poem Project
description: Students plan, build, and reflect on a project where they animate each letter of their name.
objective_bullets: Plan, build, and reflect on a project where they animate each letter of their name

title: About Me Project
description: Students plan, build, and reflect on a project where they choose their own background, sprites (characters), animation, and script to share about themselves.
objective_bullets: Plan, build, and reflect on a project where choose their own background, sprites (characters), animation, and script to share about themselves

title: Dance Party Project
description: Students plan, build, and reflect on a project where they program sprites to change costumes to create a movement effect.
objective_bullets: Plan, build, and reflect on a project where they program sprites to change costumes to create a movement effect

title: Role Model Lessons
description: Here are seven lessons about people from underrepresented groups in the computing field who have made or are making contributions to the computing field. Students explore various occupations in the computing field.
objective_bullets: Explore seven lessons about people from underrepresented groups in the computing field who have made or are making contributions to the computing field|Various occupations in the computing field

title: Environmental Literacy Systems Project- coming soon!
description: These lessons teach students that a system is two or more parts that interact to form a whole. Students learn about how trees affect people and how people affect trees. The concept of cause and effect is taught explicitly. For the Scratch project, students narrate the relationships and animate sprites in a tree connection circle.
objective_bullets: Teach that a system is two or more parts that interact to form a whole|About how trees affect people and how people affect trees|Explore cause and effect is taught explicitly|For the Scratch project, learners narrate the relationships and animate sprites in a tree connection circle

title: Growth Mindset and the Brain
description: This quick project guides students to make a digital Thanksgiving greeting card. Students animate the sprites in the project and record a message. The project can be emailed to friends and family simply by copying and pasting the URL.
objective_bullets: This quick project guides make a digital Thanksgiving greeting card|Animate the sprites in the project and record a message|The project can be emailed to friends and family simply by copying and pasting the URL

title: What is a Change-Maker?
description: Students define what a change-maker is and choose a change-maker to research and report on.
objective_bullets: Define what a change-maker is and choose a change-maker to research and report on

title: Researching Your Change-Maker
description: Students generate questions for their research. They take notes systematically.
objective_bullets: Generate questions for their research|Take notes systematically

title: Planning Your Project
description: Students animate and narrate the change-maker's story in the Scratch environment. Reflection and Sharing Students reflection the creation process and will share their project with others.
